{"assignee":"marlboro-red","status":"in_progress"}
old_value: {"id":"obelisk-frt","title":"CPR response ESC[1;1R leaks as visible text on macOS","description":"on_agent_pty_ready() in app.rs:2048 unconditionally writes \\x1b[1;1R (cursor position report) to every agent's PTY stdin. This was intended for Windows ConPTY's startup ESC[6n handshake, but on macOS no such query is sent. Claude Code receives the unexpected CPR on stdin and renders it as literal text '^[[1;1R' in the terminal output. Fix: gate the DSR response behind #[cfg(windows)] or, better, intercept ESC[6n from PTY output and respond dynamically.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"chanjun.park@wisetechglobal.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T13:02:52Z","created_by":"marlboro-red","updated_at":"2026-03-12T13:02:52Z"}

new_value: {"assignee":"marlboro-red","status":"in_progress"}
old_value: {"id":"obelisk-6l9","title":"Line count increments too fast — counts raw PTY newlines instead of visible lines","description":"total_lines in app.rs:2090 counts raw 0x0A bytes in the PTY data stream: agent.total_lines += data.iter().filter(|\u0026\u0026b| b == b'\\n').count(). Terminal apps like Claude Code frequently redraw the screen (spinners, progress bars, status updates), re-emitting newlines for the same visual lines on every refresh. This causes the line counter to inflate rapidly — often 10-100x the actual content lines. Additionally, lines_this_tick on line 2096 uses newlines.max(1), adding minimum 1 per 4KB PTY chunk even with zero newlines. The counter is displayed on the Dashboard agent list (ui.rs:1102) and the Agent Detail diagnostics panel (ui.rs:1663). Fix: derive line count from vt100 parser scrollback_len() + screen rows, which tracks actual unique content lines rather than raw byte throughput.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"chanjun.park@wisetechglobal.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T13:04:10Z","created_by":"marlboro-red","updated_at":"2026-03-12T13:04:10Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"ROOT CAUSE: spawn_agent_pty() in runtime.rs never sent the ESC[1;1R cursor-position response to ConPTY's ESC[6n DSR query on startup, causing output buffering on Windows. The integration tests already did this but the main code path did not. FIX: Added writer.write_all(b\"\\x1b[1;1R\") + flush immediately after obtaining the writer in spawn_agent_pty(). REGRESSION TEST: No new test added — the existing integration tests already cover DSR response behavior. Filed obelisk-yfq for a pre-existing test failure in pty_write_input_and_read_response on macOS."}

new_value: {"notes":"ROOT CAUSE: resize_agent_pty() replaced the vt100::Parser with a new instance on every resize, destroying all scrollback. FIX: Use parser.screen_mut().set_size(rows, cols) to resize in place, preserving buffer content. REGRESSION TEST: yes - vt100_parser_resize_preserves_scrollback."}

Should preserve scrollback content across resize events.","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"marlboro-red","owner":"chanjun.park@wisetechglobal.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T12:59:57Z","created_by":"marlboro-red","updated_at":"2026-03-12T13:01:30Z"}

new_value: Fixed: resize_agent_pty() was replacing the vt100 parser with a new instance, losing all scrollback. Now uses screen_mut().set_size() to resize in place, preserving buffer content.

Completed: Replaced 3 unwrap() calls with safe if-let alternatives — 2 on selected_agent_id and 1 on confirm_kill_agent_id in main.rs. No behavior change; UI state desync now silently skips instead of panicking.
{"assignee":"marlboro-red","status":"in_progress"}
old_value: {"id":"obelisk-a2n","title":"Add manual 'mark complete' action for agents","description":"When an agent finishes its work but the Claude Code process remains alive (idle REPL), the only option is to kill it, which sets exit code non-zero and marks it as Failed. Need a keybinding (e.g. 'D' for done) that: 1) Sets the agent status to Completed (overriding exit code detection), 2) Sends SIGTERM to clean up the process, 3) Triggers normal completion flow (worktree cleanup, session recording, bd close). This avoids the kill-shows-as-failed problem when an agent has genuinely finished but the underlying CLI session hasn't exited.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"chanjun.park@wisetechglobal.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T13:05:58Z","created_by":"marlboro-red","updated_at":"2026-03-12T13:05:58Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"COMPLETED: Added tracing + tracing-subscriber + tracing-appender. File-based logging to .beads/logs/obelisk.log with daily rotation and non-blocking writes. Instrumented startup/shutdown, agent spawn/exit, poll results, errors. DECISIONS: Used file-only logging since TUI owns stdout/stderr. Default level is info, configurable via RUST_LOG."}

Would help debugging in production without the TUI active, and could log to file alongside the terminal UI.","status":"in_progress","priority":4,"issue_type":"chore","assignee":"marlboro-red","owner":"chanjun.park@wisetechglobal.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T13:00:05Z","created_by":"marlboro-red","updated_at":"2026-03-12T13:02:45Z"}

new_value: Completed: Added tracing, tracing-subscriber, and tracing-appender for structured file-based logging. Logs to .beads/logs/obelisk.log with daily rotation, non-blocking writes, and configurable level via RUST_LOG. Instrumented startup/shutdown, agent lifecycle, polls, and errors.

{"notes":"COMPLETED: Verified LazyLock\u003cRegex\u003e optimization already in place (RE_INPUT_TOKENS, RE_OUTPUT_TOKENS at app.rs:15-20). Added 7 unit tests for token regex parsing and detect_phase. All unit tests pass. DECISIONS: No code change to regex compilation needed — LazyLock was introduced in obelisk-942 and is the correct approach."}

Compile once at startup using LazyLock or once_cell to avoid repeated overhead.","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"marlboro-red","owner":"chanjun.park@wisetechglobal.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T13:00:02Z","created_by":"marlboro-red","updated_at":"2026-03-12T13:01:28Z"}

new_value: Fixed: spawn_agent_pty() was missing the ConPTY DSR response (ESC[1;1R) that the integration tests already sent. Added writer.write_all + flush immediately after obtaining the PTY writer, matching the test pattern. This prevents output buffering on Windows when ConPTY waits for the handshake.

Completed: LazyLock<Regex> optimization was already in place (RE_INPUT_TOKENS, RE_OUTPUT_TOKENS in app.rs). Added 7 unit tests validating regex compilation, pattern matching, case insensitivity, and detect_phase ordering.

{"description":"Token parsing in on_agent_pty_data() (app.rs:2077-2087) is unreliable — ANSI escapes break regex matching, chunk boundaries split lines, and the format assumption may not match Claude Code's actual output. Rather than attempting to fix fragile PTY scraping, remove the token in/out display and cost estimation features entirely. This includes: 1) Remove RE_INPUT_TOKENS / RE_OUTPUT_TOKENS regexes and parse_token_count() (app.rs:15-24), 2) Remove token parsing from on_agent_pty_data() (app.rs:2077-2087), 3) Remove TOK IN / TOK OUT from Agent Detail diagnostics panel (ui.rs:1670-1675), 4) Remove cost estimation logic and recalculate_agent_cost() (app.rs:2210+), 5) Remove token fields from AgentInfo and SessionRecord types (types.rs:227-230, 290-292), 6) Remove TOKENS display from status bar (ui.rs:2681), 7) Remove default_model_pricing() (app.rs:26-37). Can be re-added later if runtimes provide a structured API for token usage."}
old_value:
{"id":"obelisk-b45","title":"Token in/out parsing broken — ANSI escapes and chunk boundaries break regex","description":"Token parsing in on_agent_pty_data() (app.rs:2077-2087) has multiple issues:

1. ANSI escape sequences break regex: The regex runs against raw PTY bytes which contain ANSI color/cursor codes. Claude Code renders token counts with escape sequences interspersed (e.g. ESC[38;5;240mInput tokensESC[0m: ESC[1m12,345), so the regex pattern 'input[_ ]tokens?[:\s]+([0-9][0-9,]*)' fails to match because [:\s]+ can't skip over escape codes between the label and number.

2. No ANSI stripping: There is no strip_ansi function anywhere in the codebase. Raw PTY bytes go directly to regex matching.

3. Chunk boundary splitting: Parsing runs per ~4KB PTY data chunk. If a token count line is split across two chunks, it's missed entirely.

4. Format mismatch: Claude Code's actual token output format may not match the assumed 'input tokens: N' pattern.

Fix: Strip ANSI escapes before regex matching (e.g. use the strip-ansi-escapes crate), and either buffer text across chunks or parse from the vt100 screen contents instead of raw bytes.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"chanjun.park@wisetechglobal.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T13:08:14Z","created_by":"marlboro-red","updated_at":"2026-03-12T13:08:14Z"}

Rather than attempting to fix fragile PTY scraping, remove the token in/out display and cost estimation features entirely. This includes: 1) Remove RE_INPUT_TOKENS / RE_OUTPUT_TOKENS regexes and parse_token_count() (app.rs:15-24), 2) Remove token parsing from on_agent_pty_data() (app.rs:2077-2087), 3) Remove TOK IN / TOK OUT from Agent Detail diagnostics panel (ui.rs:1670-1675), 4) Remove cost estimation logic and recalculate_agent_cost() (app.rs:2210+), 5) Remove token fields from AgentInfo and SessionRecord types (types.rs:227-230, 290-292), 6) Remove TOKENS display from status bar (ui.rs:2681), 7) Remove default_model_pricing() (app.rs:26-37). Can be re-added later if runtimes provide a structured API for token usage.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"chanjun.park@wisetechglobal.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T13:08:14Z","created_by":"marlboro-red","updated_at":"2026-03-12T13:08:56Z"}
